title: xylopicrum neglectum
intro: Xylopicrum neglectum, also known as the African star apple, is a tropical tree native to West Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The tree's bark, leaves, and roots have been used to prepare herbal remedies, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Xylopicrum neglectum include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are traditionally used in African folk medicine to promote overall well-being.
